title: Array word problems
description: Practice solving word problems by adding the same number many times.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jo made a batch of cookies. She baked $5$ rows of cookies with $4$ cookies in each row.

**Which image below shows how Jo arranged her cookies?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Jo baked $5$ rows of cookies with $4$ cookies in each row.

This array shows $\blueE5$ rows with $\purpleD4$ squares in each row.

![A group of 5 lines with 4 squares in each line. The squares are numbered at the top 1 through 4. The squares are numbered at the left 1 through 5.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4c94ab72a5e867b38381e80c8c679200a59b1dbd)

This image shows how Jo arranged her cookies: 

![A group of 5 lines with 4 squares in each line.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3a74c088605820be724fbdf7675a5309c5a9948b)

choices.content: ![A group of 5 lines with 4 squares in each line.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3a74c088605820be724fbdf7675a5309c5a9948b)
![A group of 3 lines with 5 squares in each line.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a66e9ddc613b8bb950725cd0fa23e2e94c383b84)
![A group of 2 lines with 5 squares in the first line and 4 squares in the second line.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5f5caef77409f99fb81d83c1dcf23d296f6276f4)

assessment_items.question_content:
Alex arranged his toy cars. He put them in $3$ rows with $3$ cars in each row.

**Which image below shows how Alex arranged his toy cars?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Alex arranged his cars into $3$ rows with $3$ car in each row.

This array shows $\blueE3$ rows with $\purpleD3$ squares in each row.

![A group of 3 lines with 3 squares in each line. The squares are numbered at the top 1 through 3. The squares are numbered at the left 1 through 3.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b62911228d90f8c56d1d1086ac241a5dcbfb1857)

This image shows how Alex arranged his cars: 

![A group of 3 lines with 3 squares in each line.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f861a49e2f812e66f5d60dd4ca0b270b3964d418)

choices.content: ![A group of 3 lines with 3 squares in each line.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f861a49e2f812e66f5d60dd4ca0b270b3964d418)
![A group of 3 lines with 3 squares in the first line. The second line has 1 square. The third line has 1 square.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8f4262762eba1263ff04808bf2ed23a28b420864)
![A group of 2 lines with 2 squares in each line.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4c3c0f33c34acf744cea9c0dc4f7541d2a91ae26)

assessment_items.question_content:
A squirrel was preparing for winter. She arranged her acorns by putting them in $2$ rows with $4$ acorns in each row.

**Which image below shows how the squirrel arranged her acorns?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The squirrel arranged her acorns into $2$ rows with $4$ acorns in each row.

This array shows $\blueE2$ rows with $\purpleD4$ acorns in each row.

![A group of acorns in 2 rows with 4 acorns in each row. The acorns are numbered across the top 1 through 4. The rows are numbered 1 through 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a258e419dfc603649572ea5dd8b84f3d7bdbc215)

This image shows how the squirrel arranged her acorns: 

![A group of acorns in 2 rows with 4 acorns in each row.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4bb2de0d23744350f9568c6c49ce6ca39abe9a83)

choices.content: ![A group of acorns in 2 rows with 4 acorns in each row.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4bb2de0d23744350f9568c6c49ce6ca39abe9a83)
![A group of acorns in 3 rows with 4 acorns in each row.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c46fe1e5fe22dec7527baa821aae166c7a935192)
![A group of acorns in 2 rows with 5 acorns in each row.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e277cececc6cefde1b113b1dbf71333906281b38)

assessment_items.question_content:
Nora is organizing her marbles. She put them in $4$ rows with $1$ marble in each row.

**Which image below shows how Nora organized her marbles?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Nora put her marbles into $4$ rows with $1$ marble in each row.

This array shows $\blueE4$ rows with $\purpleD1$ marble in each row.

![A group of marbles in 4 rows with 1 marble in each row. The marbles are labeled with number 1 at the top and the rows are labeled 1 through 4 at the left.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/81df4d049b757ee290afc173f7d3900d1cb74fba)

This image shows how Nora organized her marbles: 

![A group of marbles in 4 rows with 1 marble in each row.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a850ad72da6024c47359f4bdc24e3c05fd90d6d4)

choices.content: ![A group of marbles in 4 rows with 1 marble in each row.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a850ad72da6024c47359f4bdc24e3c05fd90d6d4)
![A group of marbles in 4 rows with 4 marbles in each row.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6733f8076d0eaa4c3a9cffa00317a15e4ab98df6)
![A group of marbles in 5 rows with 1 marble in each row.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0f78a1585cc2cd660c4f0b4a9587a2f8207c484e)

assessment_items.question_content:
Hal put his jellybeans into $3$ rows with $4$ jellybeans in each row.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/90690be3140296dcdd775ed7b05db25dfb720728)

 **Which expression could we use to find the total number of jellybeans?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\blueE3$ rows with $\purpleD4$ circles in each row.  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ee34b8f38f15e3b18fa2342395d8cc2f90388bd1)

We can relate the array to repeated addition:

$\overset{\blueE1}{\purpleD{4}} + \overset{\blueE2}{\purpleD{4}} +  \overset{\blueE3}{\purpleD{4}}$
$\blueE3$ groups of $\purpleD4$ equals ${\purpleD{4}} +{\purpleD{4}} + {\purpleD{4}}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Lulu was packing for a trip. She put her clothes inside of her suitcase in $2$ rows with $5$ shirts in each row.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/452905c0b7ca6515b0b12627e20c2e2d43de2363)

 **Which expression could we use to find the total number of shirts Lulu packed?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\blueE2$ rows with $\purpleD5$ shirts in each row.  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/247f0cc15845e08d1db90bc6d2f44937c198c192)

We can relate the array to repeated addition:

$\overset{\blueE1}{\purpleD{5}} + \overset{\blueE2}\purpleD{5}$
$\blueE2$ groups of $\purpleD5$ equals $\purpleD{5} + \purpleD{5}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Abe was making s'mores. He put his marshmallows on a plate in $2$ rows with $3$ marshmallows in each row.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9645db4e27a7084d9712ad940aa0ef1d564446b9)

 **Which expression could we use to find the total number of marshmallows Abe has for making s'mores?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\blueE2$ rows with $\purpleD3$ marshmallows in each row.  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/107a2e4bc85de0eabe877290bd94b5ff00afbb1b)

We can relate the array to repeated addition:

$\overset{\blueE1}{\purpleD{3}} + \overset{\blueE2}\purpleD{3}$
$\blueE2$ groups of $\purpleD3$ equals $\purpleD{3} + \purpleD{3}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kate put all of her dog's bones into rows. She put the bones in $5$ rows with $1$ dog bone in each row.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cca913d5fedd2935c8bf11e84f6c967aa897f3b3)

 **Which expression could we use to find the total number of dog bones Kate has?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
There are $\blueE5$ rows with $\purpleD1$ dog bone in each row.  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/90cf2e309445b7a0d101b2f9e869bdb6b8202852)

We can relate the array to repeated addition:

$\overset{\blueE1}{\purpleD{1}} + \overset{\blueE2}\purpleD{1} + \overset{\blueE3}{\purpleD{1}} + \overset{\blueE4}\purpleD{1} + \overset{\blueE5}\purpleD{1}$
$\blueE5$ groups of $\purpleD1$ equals $\purpleD{1} + \purpleD{1} + \purpleD{1} + \purpleD{1} + \purpleD{1}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ted baked some cookies. He put the cookies in $2$ rows with $3$ cookies in each row.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/eeaffe39a33e243f0a9f9fbaff10cf7cc44bfd25)

**Use the image above to help you find the total number of cookies. **

$3 + 3 ={}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

There are [[☃ numeric-input 2]] cookies in all.
hints:
There are $\blueE2$ rows of $\purpleD3$.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/22027048d6096c0d6891e0c4f54f03f3a47a3e06)

So, there are $\blueE2\purpleD{\text{ threes}}$.

$\overset{\blueE1}{\purpleD{3}} + \overset{\blueE2}{\purpleD{3}} = \maroonD{6}$
There are $\maroonD6$ cookies in all.

assessment_items.question_content:
Lily wanted to make some biscuits. She put the biscuits in $4$ rows with $3$ biscuits in each row.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e2260e97f3c6f4100dcf2bf30e6667c1e90301af)

**Use the image above to help you find the total number of biscuits. **

$3 + 3 +3 + 3 ={}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

There are [[☃ numeric-input 2]] biscuits in all.
hints:
There are $\blueE4$ rows of $\purpleD3$.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8715b6e2217cbce774e15c16cb6fd5b666f4979e)

So, there are $\blueE4\purpleD{\text{ threes}}$.

$\overset{\blueE1}{\purpleD{3}} + \overset{\blueE2}{\purpleD{3}} + \overset{\blueE3}{\purpleD{3}} + \overset{\blueE4}{\purpleD{3}} = \goldE{12}$
There are $\goldE{12}$ biscuits in all.

assessment_items.question_content:
Easton is organizing his bug collection. He put the bugs in $5$ rows with $2$ bugs in each row.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0097b52e85abf1aee6e7440f2e171594aca04c73)

**Use the image above to help you find the total number of bugs. **

$2 + 2 +2 +2+2 ={}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

There are [[☃ numeric-input 2]] bugs in all.
hints:
There are $\blueE5$ rows of $\purpleD2$.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0d5e0cd95541d50cfee46df0aa850de6032abee4)

So, there are $\blueE5\purpleD{\text{ twos}}$.

$\overset{\blueE1}{\purpleD{2}} + \overset{\blueE2}{\purpleD{2}} + \overset{\blueE3}{\purpleD{2}} + \overset{\blueE4}{\purpleD{2}} +\overset{\blueE5}{\purpleD{2}} = \goldE{10}$
There are $\goldE{10}$ bugs in all.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jim is organizing his sticker collection. He put the stickers in $3$ rows with $1$ sticker in each row.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a03fccc905542f7d0c91d6b5c32322b39697bc6e)

**Use the image above to help you find the total number of stickers. **

$1 + 1 + 1 ={}$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

There are [[☃ numeric-input 2]] stickers in all.
hints:
There are $\blueE3$ rows of $\purpleD1$.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b347084a57076ca578b777e3e4d7cff47d44864c)

So, there are $\blueE3\purpleD{\text{ ones}}$.

$\overset{\blueE1}{\purpleD{1}} + \overset{\blueE2}{\purpleD{1}} + \overset{\blueE3}{\purpleD{1}} = \redE{3}$
There are $\redE{3}$ stickers in all.
